title: Release governance, inherited safety evidence, and rollout boundary
summary: Phase 5 now has a full executable and browser-proof foundation, but inherited safety, rollback, and widened-rollout constraints still prevent an unconditional approval verdict.

title: Acting context and cross-organisation visibility
summary: Acting-context drift, minimum-necessary visibility, and break-glass posture all fail closed across backend and browser-visible surfaces.

title: Capacity ingestion and source trust
summary: Source admission, degraded/quarantined handling, and local twin partner feeds are proven, but supported-test and live onboarding still remain bounded operational work.

title: Candidate ranking and alternative-offer truth
summary: The visible frontier, warned-choice treatment, and cross-surface truth are proven, while the queue-side expiry timer integration remains an explicit fail-closed gap.

title: Hub claim, transfer, and ownership fence
summary: Ownership epochs, stale-owner recovery, transfer continuity, and same-shell queue claim behavior remain materially intact.

title: Commit truth and confirmation evidence
summary: Commit fencing, pending-versus-confirmed truth, imported confirmation handling, and browser confirmation surfaces are all backed by current proof.

title: Reminder/manage and patient timeline continuity
summary: Manage capability settlement, reminders, and merged appointment-family continuity are all proven without misleading calmness.

title: Practice visibility and acknowledgement debt
summary: Practice visibility projections and acknowledgement debt remain separate truths, and the browser-visible surfaces keep the distinction intact.

title: MESH continuity, route verification, and partner-route setup
summary: Local-twin continuity, route verification, and supported environment separation are proven, but lawful live onboarding remains a controlled manual or review boundary.

title: No-slot, callback transfer, return-to-practice, and reopen recovery
summary: Fallback, callback, urgent return, and reopen behavior are proven as distinct authorities rather than flattened into one recovery outcome.

title: Browser accessibility, responsive parity, and content quality
summary: The final browser-visible wave is proved for truth, accessibility, focus order, and responsive continuity across the relevant hub and patient surfaces.
